info.title: Sonik Booking Service API
info.description:
Generated contract truth for the Sonik booking service.

This is the booking surface denominator, not the legacy Node API inventory. Operations expose machine-readable x-sonik-status and x-sonik-adapter metadata:
- mounted/mounted: served by sonik-booking-service today.
- shadow/not-mounted: typed contract truth for future booking families; not mounted by runtime yet.

The document is generated from oRPC contract metadata, stamped with Better Auth session security, verified booking error rows, and committed fixture examples. Do not hand-edit the generated OpenAPI document.

tags.name: booking
tags.description: Greenfield booking service contracts. Mounted operations are live; shadow operations are future contract truth only.

tags.name: booking-templates
tags.description: Public read-only organizer template manifests and dry-run/browser handoff reports; no install mutation is mounted here.

tags.name: booking-media
tags.description: Authenticated private media assets for bookable contexts. Backed by BOOKING_MEDIA_BUCKET; public CDN rendering remains deferred.

get.description: Liveness probe for the greenfield booking service. Returns service identity, ok flag, session source, explicit demo-auth posture, demoCustomerId for local/demo harness proof, venueProductionReady=false, and timestamp. No authentication required — anonymous callers receive authenticated: false. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service router.ts.

ok.description: Liveness flag — always true on a 200.

authenticated.description: Whether a booking session resolved for this request (false when no session token or demo harness is present).

sessionMode.description: Resolved session source. demo-harness/dev-header are local/demo-only and are not venue-production auth.

demoAuth.description: True only when the explicit local/demo auth harness resolved this request.

demoCustomerId.description: Deterministic demo user id exposed through the legacy demoCustomerId field only by the local/demo harness so demo proofs can commit user-bound holds without claiming CRM ownership.

venueProductionReady.description: Always false until the Amplify production session adapter is configured, command-policy gates are mounted, and venue smoke is complete.

at.description: Server timestamp (ISO 8601) when the probe was handled.

schema.description: Booking service health probe payload (see BookingContractCompatibility.pingShape).

post.description: Creates a bookable context — the managed bookable thing (an event, a venue_schedule like a tee sheet or restaurant service, or a resource like a simulator bay). Provide its kind, display name, and timezone (required); config defaults to empty and slug is optional. The owning organization is taken from your session — never send it in the body. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

kind.description: The kind of managed bookable thing. `event` is a projected spine event; `venue_schedule` is a recurring-capacity context (restaurant service, gym timetable, tee sheet — differing only in schedule-rule config); `resource` is a book-the-thing-itself context (simulator bay, private dining room). Closed enum — set at creation, immutable thereafter.

timezone.description: IANA timezone the context's schedule is computed in (required — slot times are meaningless without it).

config.description: Operator-provided config (slot duration, capacity, windows, pacing). Optional; defaults to {} server-side.

slug.description: Optional URL-friendly handle. Not unique — duplicates are accepted by the schema.

Closed enum — set at creation, immutable thereafter.

post.description: Projects a real Sonik event into booking as bookable_context.kind=event using externalEventRef as a soft shadow key. This operation is idempotent per organization + externalEventRef, refreshes mutable display/config fields on replay, and does not make booking authoritative for legacy event ticketing, approvals, analytics, or commerce. post.description: Adds a schedule rule to a context. A rule is an open config envelope expressing an effective-date range, a priority, and pacing/window/turn-time settings (e.g. opens/closes, slotMinutes, capacity); higher-priority rules win where effective windows overlap. Rules are how availability is defined — availability itself is always computed from them, never stored. Emits an operational attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

get.summary: Get computed availability for a context
get.description: Returns the computed availability slots for a context over a date range (from/to). This is THE law of the booking framework: availability is computed from the context's schedule rules every time it is requested — it is never stored as slots. Each returned slot carries its start, end, and remaining capacity after existing bookings. Optional partySize/source/resource filters let the read hide slots that active booking-window or party policies would reject; the response remains a bare slot array without pricing or reason-code expansion. This read is NOT tracked as an attribution signal. Mounted runtime: served by sonik-booking-service booking context handlers.

schema.description: The bookable context's id.
Start of the availability window (ISO 8601 date or datetime), inclusive.

Optional party size for policy-aware availability filtering. Defaults to 1 when omitted.
Optional booking source for policy-aware availability filtering. Defaults to web when omitted.
Optional resource type target for policy-aware availability filtering. When supplied with resourceUnitId, the unit must belong to this type.
Optional resource unit target for policy-aware availability filtering. Implies the unit's resource type for policy matching.

capacityRemaining.description: Remaining capacity for this slot after existing bookings are subtracted. 0 means the slot is full but still shown (it exists in the schedule).

items.description: A single computed availability slot (synthesized from schedule rules, never stored).

schema.description: The computed availability slots for a context over the requested window, as a bare raw array. Slots are synthesized from schedule rules — never stored.

post.description: Creates a booking against a bookable context for a persistent user/guest over a time window. The same shape backs a tee time, a dinner reservation, or any other booking — the context decides what it means. Supply a clientRequestId to make the create idempotent: retrying with the same key returns the existing booking with 200 rather than creating a duplicate (the Worker-retry defense). The organization is taken from your session. Emits a v4 attribution signal carrying the source channel at capture time.

contextId.description: The bookable_context to book against (FK → bookable_context.id).

userId.description: Persistent auth/user id for the person holding the booking. Use /api/v1/booking/guests to search or create this identity.

anyOf.description: A booking-domain UUID (Postgres uuid primary/foreign key).

customerId.description: Optional/deprecated org-scoped customer-context id for imported CRM/marketing context. Never required for reservation creation.

endsAt.description: Window end — must be strictly after startsAt (422 otherwise).

source.description: Channel the booking originated from (text + CHECK, schema.ts:122-125). Carried into the v4 attribution signal at capture time.

clientRequestId.description: Idempotency key. Retrying create with the same (org, clientRequestId) returns the EXISTING booking with 200, never a duplicate. Omit for server-originated rows. See BookingBookingsContractCompatibility.idempotency.

post.summary: Post a charge, payment, or adjustment
post.description: Operator op. Records one ledger entry on a member's tab: a charge (positive — they owe more, e.g. a tee time or dinner), a payment (negative — they paid the tab down), or an adjustment (either sign — a comp or correction). amount_cents must be nonzero. Optionally links the entry to the booking that produced it. Ledger-only: this records money, it does not capture or settle any payment (no payment rails are integrated). Intended as the future Layer-1 monetary-signal emission point (capture-only). Responds with the created entry. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

get.summary: Read a member's statement / F&B-minimum progress
get.description: Operator op. Returns a computed statement for a member's tab over an optional occurred-at period: the net balance, per-kind subtotals (charges, payments, adjustments), and an entry count. The charge subtotal over a billing period is the food-and-beverage-minimum progress number — how much the member has spent toward their minimum. Every figure is a read-time aggregate over the ledger; nothing here is stored. Shadow contract: no adapter is mounted yet.

schema.description: House-account id.
Inclusive period start (ISO 8601); omit for all-time.
Exclusive period end (ISO 8601); omit for all-time.

totalCents.description: Net balance over the (optionally period-filtered) entries = SUM(amount_cents). Positive = member owes the house.

chargeCents.description: Sum of charge amounts (positive). Over a billing period this IS the F&B-minimum progress number — see fbMinimumProgress.

paymentCents.description: Sum of payment amounts (negative or zero) — how much the member has paid the tab down.

adjustmentCents.description: Net of adjustments (comps/credits negative, corrections positive).

entryCount.description: Number of entries included in the statement.

period.description: The occurred-at window the statement was computed over; absent for an all-time statement.

schema.description: A computed house-account statement / F&B-minimum-progress read (see BookingHouseAccountsContractCompatibility.fbMinimumProgress). Every figure is a read-time aggregate — never stored.
